title: Direct Routing and Bounded Worker Pools · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A state public-records accessibility unit. They need a Go work service with explicit claims, long-lived connection ownership, versioned topology, bounded goroutines, direct routing, fair consumers, context cancellation, and acknowledgement only after durable effect. Planned artifact: Deliver Bounded Public-Records Work Engine with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Assign channel ownership by publisher or consumer lifecycle and prevent concurrent confirm, acknowledgement, and topology operations from corrupting assumptions; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Recover connection, channels, topology, confirm state, prefetch, consumers, and publisher admission in generation order while rejecting stale notifications and deliveries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Outcome Evidence Contract
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go messaging team handles case 3452 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then fail the canary and prove older Go consumers remain compatible. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Outcome Evidence Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Outcome Evidence Contract, keep Outcome Evidence Contract connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

Build deterministic Go evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; reject stale connection and channel generations; then cancel the parent context while a result is still ambiguous. hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Project Worker Effect Evidence, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Worker Effect Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Project Worker Effect Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A project Go messaging team handles case 1974 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Project Worker Effect Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A worker should acknowledge before starting slow work to prevent duplicates. In Go, one goroutine per delivery is not a capacity or fairness policy.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Worker Effect Evidence happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Worker Effect Evidence condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Worker Effect Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the project evidence note for Worker Effect Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A project Go messaging team handles case 1783 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Worker Effect Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Worker Effect Evidence, keep Worker Effect Evidence connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
